CAP 33-26(I).title: CTD for CAP 33-26(I), New DD Form 1610 and DD Form 1614, dated Apr 2026
CAP 33-26(I).description: A new version of the DD Form 1610, “Request and Authorization for TDY Travel of DoD Personnel” and DD Form 1614, “Request and Authorization for DoD Civilian Permanent Duty or Temporary Change of Station Travel” have been published. Effective April 27, 2026.

MAP 06-26.title: UTD for MAP 06-26(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for NAS Fallon, NV (NV211)
MAP 06-26.description: This item reinstates the NAS Fallon, NV Military Housing Area (MHA211) as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in NAS Fallon, NV MHA 211, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item reinstates NAS Fallon, NV as a location to receive up to 60 days TLE. Effective April 8, 2026.

MAP 15-2(I).title: UTD for MAP 15-2(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification for Edwards-Palmdale, CA (CA457)
MAP 15-2(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Palmdale/Edwards AFB, CA Military Housing Area (CA457). The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective April 8, 2026.

MAP 25-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 25-26(I), Clarify Use of Non-U.S. Flag Carrier by Service Member’s Dependents When U.S. Flag Carrier is Not Willing and Able to Provide Pet Transportation
MAP 25-26(I).description: This item clarifies Joint Travel Regulations par. 050107-B2 and that Service member’s dependents my use non-U.S. flag air carriers when no U.S. flag carrier is willing and able to provide pet transportation to, from, or between an Outside the Contiguous United States (OCONUS) permanent duty station (PDS). Effective May 1, 2026.

MAP 24-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 24-26(I), Clarify Pet Transportation and Quarantine Fees for Service Member’s Dependents
MAP 24-26(I).description: This item clarifies Joint Travel Regulations par. 050107 and par. 060204 and that Service member’s dependents may be authorized pet transportation and quarantine fees reimbursement during an evacuation or permanent change of station (PCS). Effective April 1, 2026.

MAP 22-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 22-26(I), Clarify Chapter 1, Table 1-7
MAP 22-26(I).description: This item clarifies the authorizing and approval authority for Army Service Members in the Joint Travel Regulations, Chapter 1, Table 1-7. The Army Compensation Chief is the main authority in Rule 2-B. Effective April 1, 2026.

MAP 17-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 17-26(I), Clarify professional books, papers, and equipment (PBP&E) by including home-schooling materials
MAP 17-26(I).description: This item clarifies that homeschooling material, or equipment that is required for the dependent’s education is considered professional books, papers, and equipment (PBP&E); leisure material and old textbooks are still excluded from PBP&E.  As directed in the Secretary of War memo, “Homeschooling Support for Military-Connected Families,” May 15, 2025, the Department conducted a review of supports and activities for home-schooling families.  Following the review, the Office of the Under Secretary

MAP 20-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 20-26(I), Correction to Transportation Allowances While at a Safe Haven
MAP 20-26(I).description: This item clarifies Joint Travel Regulations, par. 060205-B.1a that rental vehicles are not separately reimbursable and removes Table 6-16. The local travel allowance covers all transportation costs, including rental of a vehicle. Effective March 19, 2026.

MAP 19-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 19-26(I), Clarify Transportation Allowances for Service Member Dependents at a Safe Haven
MAP 19-26(I).description: This item clarifies Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), Table 6-16 and JTR, par. 060205-B.1.a that local transportation allowances may be used for payment of a rental car, public transit, taxis, and rideshare. Effective March 18, 2026.

CAP 13-26(I).title: CTD for CAP 13-26(I), Update Temporary Quarters Subsistence Expense Methodology and FTR References
CAP 13-26(I).description: In accordance with the General Services Administration’s rewrite of the Federal Travel Regulation, dated December 8, 2025, the Temporary Quarters Subsistence Expense (TQSE) Lump Sum was eliminated and TQSE Lodgings Plus is now the only method of reimbursement. Effective April 1, 2026.

MAP 12-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 12-26(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification for Vance AFB, OK (OK236)
MAP 12-26(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Vance AFB, OK Military Housing Area (OK236). The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective March 2, 2026.

MAP 11-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 11-26(I), Update Overseas Tour Lengths for Mikkeli, Finland and correct Szczecin, Poland
MAP 11-26(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement “Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS)” as directed by Assistant Secretary of War (Manpower & Reserve Affairs) memorandum, “Establishment of an Overseas Tour Length for Military Personnel in Mikkeli, Finland,” dated February 23, 2026 as well as corrects the spelling of Szczecin, Poland. Effective February 27, 2025.

MAP 08-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 08-26(I), Clarify Elapsed Time
MAP 08-26(I).description: This item clarifies elapsed time in the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) par. 050601-B1, by including “leave.” Effective February 27, 2026.

CAP 04-26.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 04-26(S), Pet Transportation Reimbursement When Travel by Foreign Air Carrier - FY26 NDAA Section 377
CAP 04-26.description: This is a statutory (S) item implementing a mandatory provision of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026.  Section 377 directs the Secretary to authorize use of a non-U.S. flag air carrier when no U.S. flag air carrier is willing and able to provide pet transportation to, from, or between an OCONUS permanent duty station.  In accordance with Section 377, this item only applies to Service members of the Armed Forces (Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, Space Force and Coast

MAP 10-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 10-26(I), Update Overseas Tour Lengths for Bahrain
MAP 10-26(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement “Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS)” as directed by Assistant Secretary of War (Manpower & Reserve Affairs) memorandum, “Amended Exception to Policy for Tour Lengths in Bahrain,” dated February 13, 2026. Effective February 13, 2026.

MAP 67-25.title: UTD for MAP 67-25(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for Vandenberg SFB, CA
MAP 67-25.description: This item reinstates the Vandenberg SFB, CA Military Housing Area (MHA) CA026 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in Vandenberg SFB, CA MHA, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item reinstates Vandenberg SFB, CA as a location to receive up to 60 days TLE. Effective February 4, 2026.

CAP 09-26(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 09-26(I), Computation Example CE-CF-01 Correction
CAP 09-26(I).description: This item corrects incorrect meal type information in the “Meals at Location A” table of the example. This meal should be indicated as “Provided by the Government” to apply the computation example rules to only pay the incidental expense (IE) on day 9/2 of the example. Effective February 9, 2026.

Expanded_Transportation_Reimbursement_Au.title: Expanded Transportation Reimbursement Authorized for Service Members in Alaska
Expanded_Transportation_Reimbursement_Au.description: DoW and Coast Guard Service members stationed in Alaska may now receive a reimbursement for airfare when taking leave to their home of record, as authorized by the FY26 NDAA.

CAP 01-26.title: CTD for CAP 01-26(S), Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) Employee Dependent Student Travel – FY26 NDAA Sec 1111
CAP 01-26.description: This is a statutory (S) item implementing a mandatory provision of the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) for Fiscal Year (FY) 2026.  Section 1111 of the FY26 NDAA directs the Secretary to authorize dependent educational travel for employees of the Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) assigned to United States Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, without regard to whether DoDEA anticipates that the covered employee will, during the 30-day period following the scheduled date of

MAP 71-25.title: UTD for MAP 71-25(S), Expand Transportation Reimbursement for Service members Assigned to Alaska
MAP 71-25.description: This is a statutory (S) item implementing two mandatory provisions of the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) for Fiscal Year 2026 (FY26).  Section 623 and Section 7232 of the FY26 NDAA create legal authority for Coast Guard members assigned to a permanent duty station (PDS) in Alaska and for unaccompanied military Service members assigned to a PDS in Alaska, for not less than one year, to be reimbursed the cost of airfare from the member’s permanent duty station (PDS) to their home of rec

CAP 02-26.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 02-26(E), Update Administrative Weight Allowances for Incirlik, Türkiye
CAP 02-26.description: This item reinstates Household Goods (HHG) weight allowances for unaccompanied and accompanied Service members and DoW civilian employees stationed at Incirlik AB, Türkiye.  In accordance with the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), par. 051401, HHG weight allowances are determined by a Service member’s pay grade on the effective date of the Permanent Change of Station (PCS) order, and whether the Service member has dependents on the effective date of the PCS order. TAC_Outreach_Call_January_13,_2026:_Grou.title: TAC Outreach Call January 13, 2026: Group Authorizations
TAC_Outreach_Call_January_13,_2026:_Grou.description: Register to join the TAC Outreach Call on January 13, 2026 to learn about Group Authorizations.

CAP 73-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 73-25(I), CY2026 Privately Owned Vehicle (POV) Mileage Rates
CAP 73-25(I).description: This memo announces the CY2026 POV Mileage Rates and updates the Frequently Asked Questions on the DTMO website for official temporary and relocation mileage rates. This change applies to travel and relocation performed on or after January 1, 2026. Effective January 1, 2026.

CAP 74-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 74-25(I), Government Meal Rates Frequently Asked Questions
CAP 74-25(I).description: This item revises the Government Meal Rates Frequently Asked Questions on the DTMO website. Effective January 1, 2026.

MAP 72-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 72-25(I), CY2026 Dislocation Allowance (DLA) Rates
MAP 72-25(I).description: This item increases the DLA rates. The FY2026 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) did not specifically establish the annual adjustment rate for Basic Pay. Therefore, 37 U.S. Code § 1009 provides authority to use the ECI of the three-month period ending on September 30 of such the prior year of the effective date if the ECI exceeds the previous year. The Bureau of Labor Statistics has calculated an ECI of 3.8% for the period ending September 30, 2024. Effective January 1, 2026.

MAP 70-25.title: UTD for MAP 70-25(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for Moody AFB, GA
MAP 70-25.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Air Force requested a TLE extension for the Moody AFB, GA military housing area (MHA081) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing. DTS_Downtime_Thursday,_December_18.title: DTS Downtime Thursday, December 18
DTS_Downtime_Thursday,_December_18.description: DTS and EWTS will be offline on Thursday, December 18, 2025 starting at 8:00am Eastern time due to a Continuity of Operations (COOP) exercise. DTS should be back online by 4:00pm Eastern time that same day.

MAP 63-25.title: UTD for MAP 63-25(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for Vance AFB, OK
MAP 63-25.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Air Force Principal has requested a TLE extension for the Vance AFB, OK because it has determined that the military housing area (MHA) is experiencing a sudden increase of Service members assigned to that location. The authority for action is 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Effective December 4, 2025.

2025_Installation_Name_Changes.title: 2025 Installation Name Changes
2025_Installation_Name_Changes.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth orders reversal of 2023 name changes to military bases. Update travel documents to avoid issues with reimbursement if visiting affected installations.

CAP 44-25(I).description: This item updates the listed PDTATAC Computation Examples to comply with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). CAP 68-25(I).description: This item corrects a typo (Camp Mujuk, ROK, was spelled incorrectly) in the JTR Supplement, Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside of the Continental United States. Effective September 24, 2025.

CAP 58-25(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 58-25(I), Escort for Child Called as a Witness
CAP 58-25(I).description: This item clarifies an escort may be authorized to accompany a witness before a military court martial when the individual is unable to travel unattended due to age or physical condition. Effective August 28, 2025.

MAP 62-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 62-25(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification - Atlantic City, NJ (NJ196)
MAP 62-25(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Atlantic City, NJ Military Housing Area (NJ196). The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective August 20, 2025.

CAP 26-25.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 26-25(E), Restructure Table 2-24. Miscellaneous Reimbursable Expenses
CAP 26-25.description: This item creates two additional tables to specifically identify local and temporary duty travel (TDY) expenses. The expense names align with the expense list options available in the Defense Travel System. This item adds two new tables under the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), section 0204: Table 2-24a. Miscellaneous Reimbursable Expenses for Local Travel and Table 2-24b. Miscellaneous Reimbursable Expenses for TDY. Effective September 1, 2025.

MAP 61-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 61-25(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification for Edwards AFB, CA (CA457)
MAP 61-25(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Palmdale/Edwards AFB, CA Military Housing Area (CA457). The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective August 19, 2025.

MAP 59-25(I).title: UTD FOR MAP 59-25(I), Update ‘Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS)’ JTR Supplement for Korea
MAP 59-25(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement “Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS)” with updated criteria for the Republic of Korea in accordance with the July 25, 2025 ASD (M&RA) memorandum, “Change to Republic of Korea Overseas Tour Lengths for Military Personnel.” Effective August 6, 2025.

CAP 57-25(I).title: CTD FOR CAP 57-25(I), Clarify Air Freight Allowance for Civilian Employees Evacuated from OCONUS
CAP 57-25(I).description: This item clarifies that civilian employees and their dependents may receive an air freight allowance or an air freight replacement allowance for unaccompanied baggage for both ordered or authorized departure from and return to a foreign location outside the continental United States (OCONUS). See the Department of State Standardized Regulations (DSSR), Section 631 for details pertaining to Air Freight Replacement Allowance and Transportation Allowance. Effective August 1, 2025.

CAP 55-25(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 55-25(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 55-25(I).description: This item corrects miscellaneous grammatical errors and multiple hyperlinks in the Joint Travel Regulations. Effective August 1, 2025.

MAP 56-25(I).title: UTD FOR MAP 56-25(I), Update ‘Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS)’ JTR Supplement for Poznan, Poland
MAP 56-25(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement “Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS)” with updated criteria for Poznan, Poland in accordance with the July 25, 2025 ASD (M&RA) memorandum, “Exception to Assignment Policy – Poznan, Poland.” Effective July 29, 2025.

MAP 54-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 54-25(I), Clarification of Table 3-5, Rule 5 PCS period
MAP 54-25(I).description: This item clarifies JTR, Table 3-5, Rule 5, that Service members with dependents attending a professional military education or training course with a scheduled duration of less than one year are eligible for per diem equal to the single-rate Basic Allowance for Housing applicable to their pay grade and location of their permanent duty station. Effective July 15, 2025.

MAP 50-25.description: This item reinstates the Astoria, OR Military Housing Area (MHA) OR241 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). This item reinstates Astoria, OR as a location where only Coast Guard members receive up to 60 days TLE. Effective July 2, 2025.

MAP 49-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 49-25(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification for Moody AFB, GA (GA081)
MAP 49-25(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Moody AFB, GA Military Housing Area (GA081). UTD-CTD_for_MAP-CAP,_Miscellaneous_Corre.description: This item corrects miscellaneous grammatical errors, updates repealed policies and multiple hyperlinks in the Joint Travel Regulations. Effective July 1, 2025.

MAP 48-25.title: UTD for MAP 48-25(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for Laughlin AFB, TX
MAP 48-25.description: This item reinstates the Laughlin AFB, TX Military Housing Area (MHA) TX278 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). CAP 45-25(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 45-25(I), Clarify OCONUS Reduced Incidental Expense
CAP 45-25(I).description: This item clarifies the OCONUS Reduced Incidental Expense in the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR). Effective July 1, 2025.

MAP 52-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 52-25(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification for NAS Fallon, NV (NV211)
MAP 52-25(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the NAS Fallon, NV Military Housing Area (NV211). The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective June 23, 2025.

MAP 47-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 47-25(I), Clarification of Tender of Service Personally Procured Move (PPM) Reimbursement
MAP 47-25(I).description: This item clarifies when a personally procured move (PPM) is performed under the tender of service program, the monetary allowance rate is equal to 100% of the Government’s constructed “Best Value” cost. Effective June 5, 2025.

MAP 42-25.title: UTD FOR MAP 42-25(R), Temporarily Increase Personally Procured Move (PPM) Reimbursement
MAP 42-25.description: This item temporarily increases the reimbursement for a personally procured move (PPM) from 100% to 130% of the Global Household Goods Contract rate. Effective June 1, 2025.

CAP 28-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 28-25(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 28-25(I).description: This item corrects miscellaneous grammatical errors, reorganizes Table 1-1, renumbers Table 2-8, and renames the title of Table 5-85 in the Joint Travel Regulations. Effective June 1, 2025.

CAP 36-25.title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 36-25(E), Privately Owned Vehicle (POV) Definition Update
CAP 36-25.description: This item modifies the current definition of POV in Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), Appendix A. A request to update the definition and clarify that vehicles shipped as a POV are being used as the primary means of transportation at the new PDS related to the execution of Global POV Contract V (GPC V). The update shall also clarify that large RVs, such as the Class A and C, are precluded from the usage of the POV shipping and storage entitlements. Effective June 1,2025.

CAP 31-25.title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 31-25(E), Reimbursement of Multiple Travelers Renting a Vehicle and Purchasing Fuel
CAP 31-25.description: This item adds the authority JTR, par. 020209 and Table 2-8 to reimburse rental car fuel and oil expenses to additional travelers when properly listed on the Company rental documentation or rental agreement under the U.S. Government Rental Car Agreement #5, paragraph 16. Additional drivers added to the documentation may be reimbursed for fuel charges. However, all other charges identified in Table 2-8 shall remain only reimbursable to the traveler booking the rental vehicle.  Effective June 1, 2

CAP 40-25(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 40-25(I), Clarify Table 2-24 Miscellaneous Expenses not Listed Elsewhere
CAP 40-25(I).description: This item clarifies late payment fee and international transaction fee in the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), Table 2-24 Miscellaneous Expenses not Listed Elsewhere. Effective June 1, 2025.

MAP 03-25.description: This item reinstates the Hancock Field Air National Guard Base (ANGB), Syracuse, NY military housing area (MHA) NY223 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in the Hancock Field ANGB, Syracuse, NY MHA, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. CAP 34-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 34-25(I), Recertification of Moscow, Russia as a Funded Environmental and Moral Leave Locations and Destinations
CAP 34-25(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, “Funded Environmental and Moral Leave (FEML) Locations and Destinations,” to recertify Moscow, Russia as a FEML duty location, effective March 31, 2025. Effective when published on the website.

MAP 33-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 33-25(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification - Vandenberg SFB, CA (CA026)
MAP 33-25(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Vandenberg SFB, CA Military Housing Area (CA026). The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective April 15, 2025.

CAP 30-25(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 30-25(I), Clarify the 800 Mile Roundtrip Rule for Privately Owned Airplane, Boat, or Helicopter
CAP 30-25(I).description: This item clarifies that the 400 miles one way/800-miles roundtrip policy does not apply to privately owned vehicle travel by airplane, boat, or helicopter. If a traveler elects to use a privately owned airplane, boat, or helicopter, then a cost comparison is required regardless of the distance traveled to the temporary duty location. Effective May 1, 2025.

MAP 29-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 29-25(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification - Moody AFB (GA081)
MAP 29-25(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Moody AFB Military Housing Area (GA081). The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective April 3, 2025.

CAP 18-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 18-25(I), Update Multiple Countries for Authorized Consumable Goods Allowance Locations
CAP 18-25(I).description:
This item updates multiple countries in the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) Supplement, Authorized Consumable Goods Allowance Locations, to correspond to the Department of State’s List of Consumables Posts. Vladivostok, Russia is no longer a location with authorized consumable goods allowance for DoD civilian employees. The additional countries where DoD civilian employees are authorized consumable goods shipments are:
• Greenland, Nuuk
• India, Chennai
• Iraq: Baghdad, Basrah, BDSC, and Erbil
•

MAP 113-24.title: UTD for MAP 113-24(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for Naval Air Station (NAS) Fallon
MAP 113-24.description: Under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11), this item extends the end date through which Naval Air Station Fallon is a location where Service members may receive an extended temporary lodging expense (TLE) due to a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate, permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing in the Fallon, NV military housing area (MHA) NV211. Effective April 2, 2025.

DoD_Government_Travel_Charge_Card_Regula.title: DoD Government Travel Charge Card Regulations Updated April 2025
DoD_Government_Travel_Charge_Card_Regula.description: Changes to the DoD Government Travel Charge Card regulations published and effective April 1, 2025.

CAP 25-25(I).title: MAP/CAP 25-25(I), Update to Foreign National Section of Who May Use DTS
CAP 25-25(I).description: This item clarifies the use of the Defense Travel System (DTS) for airfare bookings and additional expenses that a foreign national may incur. Effective when published on the DTMO website.

CAP 22-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 22-25(I), Update DoDD 5500.07 and Joint Ethics Regulation References and Weblinks
CAP 22-25(I).description: This item replaces references and weblinks to DoD 5500.07-R (Joint Ethics Regulation) with DoDD 5500.07 and “Joint Ethics Regulation,” effective May 15, 2024. Effective April 1, 2025.

CAP 24-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 24-25(I), Designation of Ukraine as a Location for R&R Leave Transportation
CAP 24-25(I).description: This item announces the designation of the land area of Ukraine as a chargeable rest and recuperation (R&R) leave duty location. Effective March 21, 2025.

CAP 23-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 23-25(I), Per Diem Rate Update for Atlantic Undersea Test and Evaluation Center (AUTEC), Andros Island, Bahamas
CAP 23-25(I).description:
The Naval Undersea Warfare Center Detachment Atlantic Undersea Test and Evaluation Center (AUTEC), Andros Island, Bahamas requested a per diem rate update. Dining services and lodging are contracted and are available to all civilian and military travelers.
Travelers are charged the actual cost of provided services. The Department of the Navy is requesting a maximum daily per diem rate of $111.00 per day, this would encompass the increased lodging rate of $59.00, the increased meal rate of $43.00

MAP 21-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 21-25(I), Update Expedited Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension Approval Process and TLE Request Form
MAP 21-25(I).description: This item updates two Joint Travel Regulations Supplements, “Expedited TLE Extension Approval Process” and “Temporary Lodging Expense Extension Due to a Housing Shortage Request Form,” to reflect the increase from 14 days to 21 days of authorized TLE. Effective when published in the JTR Supplements webpage.

CAP 20-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 20-25(I), Update Table 2-10, Rule 3
CAP 20-25(I).description: This item aligns Joint Travel Regulations, par. 020210 and Table 2-10, “Reimbursement for Privately Owned Automobiles and Motorcycles,” Rule 3 with Table 2-11, “Cost Comparison Rules for Using a POV.” This item clarifies that if a privately owned vehicle is used, the traveler is authorized mileage and related travel expenses (e.g., tolls), limited to the policy constructed cost of the transportation and other related costs associated with the authorized transportation option.  Effective April 1,

MAP 109-24.title: UTD for MAP 109-24(E), Clarify Occasional Lodging Reimbursement for ADT
MAP 109-24.description: This item clarifies that Reserve Component (RC) members performing active-duty training (ADT) when no per diem or AEA is payable, in accordance with Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) paragraph 032302-A, may be authorized reimbursement of lodging costs as occasional lodging in accordance with JTR paragraph 020305-A. The intent is to ensure RC members are reimbursed for the cost of Government quarters or Integrated Lodging Program facilities located on the installation during Annual Training and othe

CAP 19-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 19-25(I), Update Table 2-24, Rule 10
CAP 19-25(I).description: This item removes the text pertaining to the authorization to use a Regular (blue/tourist) Passport when on official travel per passport issuance/approving authority identified in DoDM-O 1000.21. The Regular Passport must not be used for clearing immigration when entering or exiting a foreign country, unless explicitly authorized in the applicable DoD Foreign Clearance Guide country page. Effective April 1, 2025.

2025_Installation_Name_Changes_-_Update_.title: 2025 Installation Name Changes - Update Travel Documents
2025_Installation_Name_Changes_-_Update_.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth orders reversal of 2023 name changes to military bases. Update travel documents to avoid issues with reimbursement if visiting affected installations.

CAP 03-24.title: CTD for CAP 03-24(R), Authority to Negotiate a Service Agreement
CAP 03-24.description: This item allows retired or separated Service members to negotiate a service agreement if appointed to a position at a non-foreign OCONUS duty station. Effective March 12, 2025.

New_Reimbursement_Available_for_Pet_Tran.title: New Reimbursement Available for Pet Transportation Costs
New_Reimbursement_Available_for_Pet_Tran.description: Effective February 21, 2025, Service members who are in receipt of PCS orders and are unable to take their pet to their next permanent duty station (PDS) may be authorized reimbursement for the cost of pet transportation to an alternative location or designated place.

Civilian_Government_Travel_Charge_Card_S.title: Civilian Government Travel Charge Card Suspension
Civilian_Government_Travel_Charge_Card_S.description: Information regarding the memorandum "Guidance for the Use of Government Travel Charge Cards by DoD Civilian Employees”

CAP 17-25(I).title: CTD for CAP 17-25(I), Update GSA Waiver Authority for Storage in Transit Extensions
CAP 17-25(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations, in accordance with the General Services Administration's waiver authority for storage in transit extensions, January 22, 2025. Effective April 1, 2025.

MAP 88-24.title: UTD for MAP 88-24(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for Bath, ME
MAP 88-24.description: This item reinstates the Bath, ME Military Housing Area (MHA) ME136 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in Bath, ME MHA, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item extends the end date through which Bath, ME is a location where Service members may receive extended TLE. Effective February 25, 2025.

CAP 16-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 16-25(I), Add INDOPACOM and SOUTHCOM to Authorized Funded Environmental and Moral Leave (FEML) Locations and Destinations
CAP 16-25(I).description: This item inserts the Republic of Palau, Los Condores Air Base/Iquique, Chile and Mendoza, Argentina to the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) Supplement “Authorized Funded Environmental and Morale Leave (FEML) Locations and Destinations.” Effective February 27, 2025.

CAP 15-25(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 15-25(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 15-25(I).description: This item corrects a minor table structure and grammatical errors in the Joint Travel Regulations. This change affects par. 050107 and Table 5-1.a. Effective on March 1, 2025.

MAP 47-24.title: UTD for MAP 47-24(R), Transportation of a Pet to or from an Alternate Location or a Designated Place
MAP 47-24.description: This item adds the authority for a Service member to be reimbursed the expenses of transporting a pet to and from a location other than the permanent duty station. Effective on February 21, 2025.

CAP 14-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 14-25(I), Clarify Constructed Travel for POV Use
CAP 14-25(I).description: This item clarifies that when a traveler uses a privately owned vehicle (POV) instead of the authorized or directed mode of transportation, they are reimbursed the POV mileage rate and related travel expenses, not to exceed the total constructed cost of the authorized transportation mode. This aligns the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) with the language contained in the Federal Travel Regulation § 301-10.309. Effective March 1, 2025.

MAP/CAP_Memo_for_Per_Diem_Updates_to_Gua.title: MAP/CAP Memo for Per Diem Updates to Guam, Northern Mariana Islands, and Wake Island
MAP/CAP_Memo_for_Per_Diem_Updates_to_Gua.description:
Per diem rates for the continental United States and foreign areas outside the continental United States (OCONUS) locations are set by the General Services Administration and Department of State, respectively. Per diem rates for all non-foreign OCONUS locations (Alaska, Hawaii, and U.S. territories and possessions) are determined by the Per Diem, Travel and Transportation Allowance Committee (PDTATAC). 

PDTATAC staff members collected contract price data from Wake Island and Midway Islands as w

CAP 11-25(I).title: CTD for CAP 11-25(I), Update Flat Payment Miscellaneous Expense Allowance (MEA) Rates
CAP 11-25(I).description: In accordance with Federal Travel Regulation (FTR) Bulletin 25-05, this item updates the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), Table 5-80 to provide the new relocation MEA flat rate amounts of $905 or the equivalent of one week’s basic gross pay, whichever is the lesser amount, for an employee without immediate family members relocating with them, and $1,810 or the equivalent of two week’s basic gross pay, whichever is the lesser amount, for an employee with immediate family members relocating with th

MAP 13-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 13-25(I), Update ‘Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS)’ JTR Supplement for Korea
MAP 13-25(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement “Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside the Continental United States (OCONUS)” with updated criteria for Korea in accordance with the February 11, 2025 ASD (M&RA) memorandum “Change to Republic of Korea Tour Length Guidance”. Effective February 11, 2025.

MAP 09-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 09-25(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification – Atlantic City, NJ (NJ196)
MAP 09-25(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Atlantic City, NJ (NJ196) Military Housing Area. The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. CAP 110-24(I).description: This item restructures Joint Travel Supplement AP-HHG-01 and Computation Examples: CF-01, CPDT-07, CPDT-33, EMLV-01, EMLV-02, EMLV-04, FEML-02, LT-01, LT-04, LTTDY-01, LTTDY-04, MPDT-07, MPDT-09, MPDT-10, MPDT-11, MPDT-16, SH-01, and SH-02 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29U.S.C. §794d). Effective when published to the DTMO Website.

Policy_Update_on_Constructed_Travel_Work.title: Policy Update on Constructed Travel Worksheet and Mileage Requirements
Policy_Update_on_Constructed_Travel_Work.description: The JTR has been amended to reinstate the rule that requires only a Constructed Travel Worksheet (CTW) for one-way mileage exceeding 400 miles.

MAP 07-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 07-25(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification Edwards AFB (CA457)
MAP 07-25(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Edwards AFB (CA457) Military Housing Area. The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective February 4, 2025.

MAP 08-25(I).title: UTD for MAP 08-25(I), Reestablish Travel for Non-Covered Assisted Reproductive Technology (ART)
MAP 08-25(I).description: This item reestablishes the authority to travel for Non-Covered Assisted Reproductive Technology (ART). Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness Memorandum “Non-Covered Assisted Reproductive Technology,” February 3, 2025, stated the rescission of Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness Memorandum, “Administrative Absence for Non-Covered Reproductive Health Care,” February 16, 2023, should not be interpreted as the elimination of access to non-covered Assisted Reprodu

CAP 05-25.title: UTD-CTD for MAP-CAP 05-25(S), Implementing Executive Order, Unleashing American Energy
CAP 05-25.description: In accordance with Executive Order of January 20, 2025, Unleashing American Energy (TAB B), this revision (TAB A) removes travel policies mandated by Executive Order 14057 of December 8, 2021, Catalyzing Clean Energy Industries and Jobs Through Federal Sustainability, Federal Travel Regulation (FTR) Bulletin 24-02, and Office of Management and Budget (OMB) M-24-05. Effective January 31, 2025.

CAP 06-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 06-25(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 06-25(I).description: This item corrects a minor table number structure in the Joint Travel Regulations, Table 6-21 and hyperlinks computation example 3 and computation example 4 in par. 0503. Affects par., 020314, 0503, and Table 6-21. Effective February 1, 2025.

CAP 02-25(I).title: CTD for CAP 02-25(I), Reinstate Temporary Integrated Lodging Program (ILP) Exemption
CAP 02-25(I).description: In accordance with the FY25 NDAA, Section 652 this item reinstates the temporary exemption for a civilian employee traveling for the performance of mission functions of a public shipyard of the DoD from the mandatory use of the Integrated Lodging Program. Effective February 1, 2025.

MAP 04-25.title: UTD for MAP 04-25(S), Remove Travel for Non-Covered Reproductive Health Care Services
MAP 04-25.description: In accordance with Executive Order, Enforcing the Hyde Amendment, January 24, 2025, this item removes Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), paragraph 033013. This item also removes definitions and acronyms related to the authority in JTR, par. 033013. Effective on January 28, 2025.

CAP 107-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 107-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 6 Tables - 508C
CAP 107-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) Tables 6-1 through 6-26 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective February 1, 2025.

MAP 79-24.title: UTD for MAP 79-24(R), Temporary Lodging Expense Extension for Vandenberg SFB, CA
MAP 79-24.description: This item reinstates the Vandenberg SFB, CA Military Housing Area (MHA) CA026 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). This item reinstates Vandenberg SFB, CA as a location to receive up to 60 days TLE. Effective on January 15, 2025.

CAP 106-24.title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 106-24(E), Clarify Other Distance Determinations
CAP 106-24.description: This item clarifies that paper or electronic standard highway mileage guides, in addition to odometer readings, may be used to determine distances outlined in the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) paragraph 020204-B. This change aligns the JTR with the Federal Travel Regulation § 301-10.302. Effective February 1, 2025.

MAP 108-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 108-24(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification – Moody AFB, GA (GA081)
MAP 108-24(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Moody AFB GA (GA081) Military Housing Area (MHA). The initiating local command documented and certified the continued existence of a housing shortage. Effective January 16, 2025.

MAP 105-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 105-24(I), Update Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty OCONUS
MAP 105-24(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside of the Continental United States (OCONUS) for Central African Republic and adds a note to Shariki, Japan. The Department of the Army memorandums dated February 11, 2022, and November 5, 2008, pursuant to authority in DoD Instruction (DoDI) 1315.18 “Procedures for Military Personnel Assignments,” authorizes Army personnel assigned to Central African Republic (CAR) and Shariki, Japan to serve a 12-mon

CAP 39-24.title: CTD for CAP 39-24(R), Removal of Temporary Quarters Subsistence Expenses Actual Expense (TQSE (AE))
CAP 39-24.description: On May 7, 2024, the General Services Administration (GSA) published new  regulations in the Federal Travel Regulation (FTR) affecting Title 41 of the Code of Federal  Regulations (CFR) Parts 300-2, 302-6, and 302-17. These changes established Temporary  Quarters Subsistence Expenses “Lodgings Plus” (TQSE (LP)) as the preferred reimbursement  method and granted the DoD the authority to discontinue use of TQSE Actual Expense (TQSE  (AE)). The DoD has determined that TQSE (AE) is unnecessary, redun

CAP 54-24.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 54-24(R), Expansion of Reimbursable Expenses for Breast Milk Shipments Related to Special Needs
CAP 54-24.description: To authorize reimbursement of disposable storage bags or non-durable containers, cold shipping packages, and refrigeration as part of the special accommodation for the shipment of breast milk expressed by a Service member or civilian employee traveling under TDY orders or a Service member traveling under PCS orders.  This aligns the Joint Travel Regulations with the authority outlined in the Federal Travel Regulation (FTR), Part 301-13, as determined by FTR Bulletin 22-03 and reenforced by Chris

CAP 01-25(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 01-25(I), Update to Dual Lodging Approval Authority
CAP 01-25(I).description: This item updates Table 2-16 adding the Assistant Secretary of Defense for Special Operations/Low Intensity Conflict (ASD SO/LIC) as the approval authority of dual lodging request which exceed 7 days for special operations travel/missions funded under Major Force Program – 11 (MFP-11). Effective February 1, 2025.

MAP 111-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 111-24(I), CY2025 Dislocation Allowance (DLA) Rates
MAP 111-24(I).description: This item increases the DLA rates based on the basic rate of pay increase of 4.5% in accordance with the FY2025 National Defense Authorization Act. Effective January 1, 2025.

CAP 101-24(I).title: UTD-CTD for MAP/CAP 101-24(I), CY2025 Government Meal Rates Frequently Asked Questions
CAP 101-24(I).description: This item revises the Government Meal Rates Frequently Asked Questions on the DTMO website. Effective January 1, 2025.

CAP 100-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 100-24(I), CY2025 Privately Owned Vehicle (POV) Mileage Rates
CAP 100-24(I).description: This memo announces the CY2025 POV Mileage Rates and updates the Frequently Asked Questions on the DTMO website for official temporary and relocation mileage rates. This change applies to travel and relocation performed on or after January 1, 2025. Effective January 1, 2025.

MAP 71-24.title: UTD for MAP 71-24(R), Increase Reimbursement for IDT Outside the Normal Commuting Distance
MAP 71-24.description: This item increases the reimbursement of actual expenses, from $500 to $750, that the Secretary concerned may authorize or approve when Reserve Component members in the Selected Reserve are required to perform Inactive Duty Training outside the local commuting distance. This item was requested by the Army in accordance with 37 USC § 452(b)(9). Effective December 27, 2024

CAP 112-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 112-24(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 112-24(I).description: This item corrects a minor table structure in the Joint Travel Regulations. This change affects Table 4-4. Effective January 1, 2025.

CAP 102-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 102-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 5 Tables 5-62 through 5-106 - 508C
CAP 102-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) Tables 5-62 through 5-106 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective January 1, 2025.

CAP 104-24(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 104-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 5 Table 5-1 through 5-61 – 508C
CAP 104-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations Table 5-1 through Table 5-61 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective January 1, 2025.

CAP 103-24(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 103-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 3 Tables – 508 Compliant
CAP 103-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations Table 3-1 through Table 3-8 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective January 1, 2025.

CAP 99-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 99-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 4 Tables - 508C
CAP 99-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) Tables 4-1 through 4-14 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective January 1, 2025.

CAP 98-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 98-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 3 Tables 3-9 through 3-20 – 508C
CAP 98-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) Tables 3-9 through 3-20 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective January 1, 2025.

MAP 93-24.title: UTD for MAP 93-24(E), Add Juba, South Sudan to the List of Authorized Consumable Goods Allowance Locations
MAP 93-24.description: This item would amend the Joint Travel Regulations, Supplement, Consumable Goods Allowances (AP-CG-01), to authorize a consumable goods allowance for Service members assigned to Juba, South Sudan. Effective December 17, 2024.

CAP 91-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 91-24(I), Update Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty OCONUS
CAP 91-24(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside of the Continental United States (OCONUS) for locations in Turkey, in accordance with the Under Secretary of Defense (Personnel and Readiness) memorandum, dated November 15, 2024 (attached). Effective November 15, 2024.

CAP 97-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 97-24(I), Clarify Rental Vehicle Damages Are Not Reimbursable as Travel Expenses
CAP 97-24(I).description: This item clarifies that payments made by a DoD traveler to a rental car company for rental vehicle damages are not reimbursable as travel and transportation expenses. If adjudicated properly as payable, they may be reimbursed as a “Miscellaneous Payment – Employee” as outlined in the DoD Guidebook for Miscellaneous Payments, under the Employee Reimbursements section. Payments for damages may not be included for reimbursement in a travel voucher. Effective January 1, 2025.

CAP 78-24(I).title: UTD/CTD FOR MAP/CAP 78-24(I), Update Joint Travel Regulations and Per Diem Supplements 508 Compliant
CAP 78-24(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) and Per Diem supplements to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). JTR supplements under sections General, Leave, Orders, PCS & Household Goods, and Temporary Lodging Expense, and Per Diem were updated to include an accessibility report, unmerge tables, correct heading styles, and Adobe PDF tags. Effective December 10, 2024.

CAP 96-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 96-24(I), Update PDTATAC Computation Examples and JTR Supplements for Section 508 Compliance (1)
