DoD_Authorizes_Additional_7_Days_to_CONU.title: DoD Authorizes Additional 7 Days to CONUS Temporary Lodging Expense
DoD_Authorizes_Additional_7_Days_to_CONU.description: Effective November 27, 2024, the number of days that a Service member may receive Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) has increased from 14 days to 21 days when moving to a permanent duty station within the continental United States (CONUS).

MAP 66-24.title: UTD for MAP 66-24(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Period Increase
MAP 66-24.description: This item was requested by the Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness (USD(P&R)). In the Secretary of Defense's memo titled "Our Enduring Duty to America’s Service Members and Their Families," guidance was given to increase the authorized TLE timeframe by 7 days, from 14 to 21 days. Effective November 27, 2024.

MAP 73-24.title: UTD for MAP 73-24(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for Atlantic City, NJ
MAP 73-24.description: This item reinstates the Atlantic City, NJ military housing area (MHA: NJ196) as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in the Atlantic City, NJ, MHA personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item reinstates the Atlantic City, NJ MHA NJ196 as a location to receive up to 60 days TLE. Effective November 25, 2024.

MAP 52-24.title: UTD for MAP 52-24(R), Add Secretarial Process Authority to Reimburse Pet Transportation Expenses in Excess of $2,000
MAP 52-24.description: This item amends the Joint Travel Regulations and authorizes the Services, through the Secretarial Process, to reimburse up to $4,000 in pet transportation expenses for OCONUS permanent change of station travel. In extenuating circumstances, if pet transportation expenses exceed $2,000, then the Service’s Secretarial Process may approve up to $4,000. Effective November 25, 2024.

CAP 87-24(I).title: CTD for CAP 87-24(I), Temporary Waiver for Broker Fees or Real Estate Commissions When Purchasing a Residence
CAP 87-24(I).description: On October 30, 2024, the General Services Administration (GSA) published FTR Bulletin 25-03, to authorize the reimbursement of broker fees and commissions for the purchase of a residence at the civilian employee’s new permanent duty station. This revision is effective November 13, 2024 and retroactively applies to buyer broker fees or commissions incurred by a civilian employee on and after August 17, 2024.

CAP 84-24(I).title: CTD for CAP 84-24(I), Waiver of Certain Provisions for Official Relocation Travel to Locations in AL, FL, GA, NC, SC, TN, and VA impacted by Hurricane Helene and Milton
CAP 84-24(I).description: This item removes relocation allowance exceptions issued under GSA Bulletin FTR 23-08 and incorporates relocation allowance exceptions issued under GSA Bulletin FTR 25-02. Certain provisions of the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) are temporarily waived for civilian employees relocating to designated locations in Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Virginia impacted by Hurricane or Tropical Storm or Post-tropical Cyclone Helene, Hurricane Milton, or both. The

MAP 67-24.title: UTD for MAP 67-24(R), Temporary Lodging Expense Extension for Edwards AFB/Palmdale CA
MAP 67-24.description: This item reinstates the Edwards AFB/Palmdale CA Military Housing Area (MHA) CA457 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in Edwards AFB/Palmdale CA, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item reinstates Edwards AFB/Palmdale CA as a location to receive up to 60 days TLE. Effective November 6, 2024.

CAP 85-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 85-24(I), Correct Outdated Reference
CAP 85-24(I).description: This item removes reference to outdated DoDD 4500.56, “DoD Policy on the Use of Government Aircraft and Air Travel and replaces it with DoDI 4500.56, “Use of Government Aircraft and Air Travel” in JTR, paragraph 030502. Effective December 1, 2024.

CAP 86-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 86-24(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 86-24(I).description: This item corrects miscellaneous minor grammatical errors and corrects language in the Joint Travel Regulations. These changes affects pars. 033201, 033202, and 051002. Effective November 1, 2024.

MAP 20-24.title: UTD for MAP 20-24(R), Expansion of Secretarial Process Authority to Increase the Number of Roundtrips for Designated Individuals and Non-Medical Attendants
MAP 20-24.description: This item increases the number of roundtrips that may be authorized through the Secretarial Process for family members and non-medical attendants. This item amends Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), par. 033201, to authorize up to two round trips in a 60-day period for family members through the Secretarial Process and amends JTR, par. 033202, to authorize additional round trips for non-medical attendants if more than one medical attendant is authorized. This item also includes updates to statutory

CAP 38-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 38-24(I), Clarify Designated Individual is Replaced by Family Member
CAP 38-24(I).description: his item clarifies that due to statutory changes, 37 USC §452(b)(12) and (13) authorize the Secretary of Defense to provide travel and transportation to family members of Service members for their presence at a military medical facility incident to the injury or illness of members or at the repatriation of members held captive. Family member with respect to a member of the Uniformed Services is defined in 37 USC §451(a)(3). For civilian employees serving in a foreign location, 10 USC § 1599b aut

MAP 82-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 82-24(I), Clarification of Roundtrip Transportation Incurred by a Child Care Provider
MAP 82-24(I).description: This item clarifies that an eligible Service member can be reimbursed for expenses incurred by a child care provider for roundtrip commercial transportation, up to the maximum reimbursement amounts. This change affects JTR, par. 050108-B. Effective November 1, 2024.

CAP 77-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 77-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 2, Table 2-14 through Table 2-27
CAP 77-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations Table 2-14 through Table 2-27 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective November 1, 2024.

CAP 75-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 75-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 2, Table 2-1 through Table 2-13
CAP 75-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations Table 2-1 through Table 2-13 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective November 1, 2024.

CAP 74-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 74-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 1 Tables - 508 Compliant
CAP 74-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations Tables 1-1, 1-6, and 1-7 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective November 1, 2024.

MAP 83-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 83-24(I), Clarify Local Emergency Displacements and Correct the Level of Authority to Authorize a Local Emergency Displacement from a Foreign Location
MAP 83-24(I).description: This item clarifies and corrects information published in UTD for MAP 02-24-(R), “Evacuation Allowances for Local Emergency Displacements”. Therefore, JTR, Table 6-3 has been changed to list the administering authorities as the Secretary of Defense, Secretary concerned, Head of the Component (or his or her designee), Commander of the installation or the Coast Guard District Commander, or Commander, head, chief, or supervisor of the organization or office. Further, JTR, par. 060101-C has been cha

MAP 63-24.title: UTD for MAP 63-24(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension for Moody AFB, GA
MAP 63-24.description: This item reinstates the Moody AFB, GA Military Housing Area (MHA) GA081 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in Moody AFB, GA MHA, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item reinstates Moody AFB, GA as a location to receive up to 60 days TLE. Effective October 18, 2024.

CAP 80-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 80-24(I), Clarify POV Use During Evacuation and Evacuation Related TDY
CAP 80-24(I).description: This item clarifies that an Authorizing or Approving Official (AO) may authorize privately owned vehicle (POV) use as advantageous to the Government without the use of a constructed cost comparison or constructed travel worksheet for evacuation travel or evacuation related temporary duty (TDY) travel. It also clarifies if an AO authorizes POV use for evacuation travel as advantageous to the Government, it is automatically considered advantageous for travel returning to the permanent duty station

MAP 68-24.title: UTD for MAP 68-24(E), Clarification of Entitlements for Retirement and Separation Travel
MAP 68-24.description: This item clarifies that entitlement to travel and transportation allowances for eligible retiring and separating Service members is based on two things, the first is the Service member is issued orders for any of the categories listed in JTR par. 051001, and the second is that the Service member and/or dependents must actually travel to the appropriate location identified in JTR section 0510. Effective November 1, 2024.

CAP 76-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 76-24(I), Restructure Joint Travel Regulations Chapter 1 Tables 1-2 and 1-4 – 508 Compliant
CAP 76-24(I).description: This item restructures the Joint Travel Regulations Tables 1-2 and 1-4 to bring them into compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. §794d). Effective November 1, 2024.

MAP 72-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 72-24(I), Update Coast Guard Office Due to Reorganization
MAP 72-24(I).description: Due to a reorganization of CG-1, this item updates CG-13 and CG-133 to CG-1M1. Effective September 27, 2024.

DoD_to_Reimburse_Transportation_Costs_of.title: DoD to Reimburse Transportation Costs of Child Care Providers
DoD_to_Reimburse_Transportation_Costs_of.description: On October 1, 2024, the Defense Department will launch a new pilot program aimed at reducing out-of-pocket expenses for Service members and their families on permanent change of station (PCS) orders.

MAP 62-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 62-24(I), Clarify POV Shipment is Limited to One Vehicle IAW Updated Statutes
MAP 62-24(I).description: This item clarifies that in accordance with 37 USC §453(c)(2) vehicle shipment may not exceed one privately owned vehicle per member household. This clarification is in conjunction with MAP 28-24(R), “Sunset of Rental Vehicle Reimbursement when POV Shipment is Delayed.” Effective October 1, 2024.

CAP 70-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 70-24(I), Clarification of Rental Vehicle Insurance Reimbursement for Certain Classified Special Operations
CAP 70-24(I).description: This item clarifies that rental vehicle insurance is reimbursable for certain special classified special operations when the nature of the mission does not allow the traveler to rent a vehicle under the U.S. Government Rental Car Agreement. The Joint Travel Regulations previously stated “…in the CONUS or non-foreign areas OCONUS” as a result of the 2017 JTR rewrite. The U.S. Government Accountability Office decision B-204486 dated January 19, 1982 is the basis for this reimbursement and does not

CAP 50-24.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 50-24 (E), Expansion of Rental Vehicle Reimbursable Expenses
CAP 50-24.description: This item updates Joint Travel Regulations Table 2-8 to add cleaning fees for rental vehicles as a reimbursable expense when the AO determines the fee is due to use of the vehicles in conjunction with the mission. Fees charged due to the traveler’s negligence or misuse of the vehicle are not reimbursable. The item also adds roadside assistance fees as an expense an AO may authorize or approve when a traveler has an emergency beyond their control with a rental vehicle such as a flat tire. Items c

CAP 61-24(I).title: CTD for CAP 61-24(I), Clarify Authorized Travel Time Rules for Civilian PDT
CAP 61-24(I).description: This item corrects Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) Table 5-72, Rule 5, for civilian employees who drive an alternative fuel privately owned vehicle to the new permanent duty station (PDS) and require additional time to travel. The authorized travel time is the actual distance traveled or actual travel time used to the new PDS. Effective September 1, 2024.

MAP 60-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 60-24(I), Multiple Temporary Lodging Expense Recertifications
MAP 60-24(I).description: This item extends the temporary lodging expense (TLE) recertification dates for the Atlantic City, NJ (NJ196), Palmdale/Edwards AFB, CA (CA457), and Moody AFB, GA (GA081) Military Housing Area. The initiating local command documented and certified the continued existence of a housing shortage. Effective August 27, 2024.

MAP 32-24.title: UTD for MAP 32-24(E), Change Time Limitation for POV Transportation in Connection with Retirement
MAP 32-24.description: This item changes the time limitation in the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), Table 5-65 for privately owned vehicle (POV) transportation in connection with retirement, placement on the temporary disability retired list (TDRL), discharge with severance or separation pay, or involuntary release from activity duty with readjustment or separation pay. Currently, Service members are authorized to extend their POV transportation for up to six years but are required to request an extension annually. Un

CAP 46-24.title: CTD for CAP 46-24(E), Clarification when Authorizing Temporary Quarters Subsistence Expenses and House Hunting Trip
CAP 46-24.description: This item amends the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) to align JTR, pars. 054008, 054206, and 054207 with Federal Travel Regulation § 302–6.30. This item clarifies that the Authorizing Official must reduce the total number of days civilian employees are authorized for TQSE (Lodging Plus) and TQSE (Actual Expense) by the number of house hunting trip days. Effective September 1, 2024.

CAP 59-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 59-24(I), Update Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty OCONUS for Powidz, Poznan, and Zagan, Poland
CAP 59-24(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside of the Continental United States (OCONUS) for locations in Powidz, Poznan, and Zagan, Poland. Military and civilian personnel are authorized to serve a 24 month accompanied tour in Poznan with adult dependents 18 years and older. Military personnel are authorized to serve a 12 month unaccompanied tour and civilian personnel are authorized to serve a 24 month unaccompanied tour. Powidz and Zagan lack

MAP 58-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 58-24(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification – Bath, ME (ME136)
MAP 58-24(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Bath, ME (ME136) Military Housing Area. The initiating local command documented and certified the continued existence of a housing shortage. Effective August 15, 2024.

MAP 51-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 51-24(I), Clarification of Permanent Duty Travel for Service Members
MAP 51-24(I).description: This item clarifies in paragraph 020310 and Table 5-5 that the per diem locality rate used for permanent change of station (PCS) computations is the next official point that requires overnight lodging. For PCS travel crossing the international dateline (IDL) this item directs the reader to two new IDL computation examples specific to PCS travel. This item also adds clarification to Table 5-3 that a port of embarkation or debarkation is used in separating legs of travel for determination of trave

CAP 56-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 56-24(I), Clarify Laundry Reimbursement is not Authorized In CONUS
CAP 56-24(I).description: This item clarifies that laundry reimbursement is not authorized within CONUS. Laundry and dry-cleaning expenses are a personal expense and are not reimbursable as part of per diem, AEA, or a miscellaneous reimbursable expense within CONUS. Effective immediately.

MAP 55-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 55-24(I), Clarify Maximum Reimbursement Amount For Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE)
MAP 55-24(I).description: This item clarifies that all lodging taxes and mandatory fees are included in the maximum TLE reimbursement amount. A Service member and his or her dependent may be reimbursed up to a maximum of $290 per day. Effective September 1, 2024.

CAP 53-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 53-24(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 53-24(I).description: This item corrects miscellaneous minor grammatical errors and corrects language in the Joint Travel Regulations. Effective August 1, 2024.

Updated_CDC_Pet_Dog_Importation_Regulati.title: Updated CDC Pet Dog Importation Regulation Goes into Effect August 1
Updated_CDC_Pet_Dog_Importation_Regulati.description: Travelers planning to enter the United States with a dog should review the impacts of the updated CDC importation regulation. Most travelers will just need to complete an online form.

CAP 48-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 48-24(I), Clarification for POV Constructed Cost Comparisons
CAP 48-24(I).description: This item clarifies that when a traveler chooses to use a Privately Owned Vehicle (POV) for TDY travel, it is considered a personal choice and is never a standard or directed mode. It also clarifies that a constructed travel worksheet (CTW) such as the DTMO’s CTW or a similar locally-approved form is required when a POV is used unless the directed/authorized mode was an available Government automobile. The AO must review the CTW to consider both cost and other factors when determining if POV use

CAP 49-24(I).title: UTD-CTD for MAP-CAP 49-24(I), Clarify When Government Quarters may be Directed
CAP 49-24(I).description: This item clarifies that Service members and civilian employees may be directed to use Government contracted or owned lodging when assigned to a contingency operation or operational deployment or performing duty under similar conditions. Effective immediately.

MAP 44-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 44-24(I), Correction to Medical Specialty Care Travel
MAP 44-24(I).description: This item further clarifies the eligibility requirements for non-emergency specialty care. Effective when published in the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR).

CAP 43-24(I).title: UTD-CTD for MAP/CAP 43-24(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 43-24(I).description: This item corrects miscellaneous minor grammatical errors and corrects language regarding house hunting trip (HHT) and temporary quarters subsistence expenses (TQSE) in the JTR. Additionally, this item corrects the definition of “Special Needs” in Appendix A. Effective July 1, 2024.

CAP 35-24.title: CAP 35-24(S), Redefining Temporary Quarters Subsistence Expenses (TQSE) Allowances
CAP 35-24.description: This item implements a mandatory provision of the Federal Travel Regulation affecting Title 41 of the Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Parts 300-2, 302-6, and 302-17, issued by the General Services administration on May 7, 2024. This item inserts changes to the Temporary Quarters Subsistence Expenses (TQSE) allowances and establishes TQSE Lodgings Plus (LP) as the preferred reimbursement method. Effective July 1, 2024.

CAP 31-24.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 31-24(E), Add Jordan, Joint Training Center, as an Administrative Household Goods (HHG) Weight Allowance Location
CAP 31-24.description: This item adds Jordan, Joint Training Center, as an administrative Household Goods (HHG) Weight Allowance Location. In accordance with the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), paragraph 051402, household goods (HHG) weight allowances can be administratively reduced at a permanent duty station (PDS) outside the continental United States (OCONUS) based on conditions at that location. Effective on June 18, 2024.

MAP 40-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 40-24(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification – Edwards AFB/Palmdale, CA
MAP 40-24(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Edwards AFB/Palmdale CA (CA457) Military Housing Area (MHA). The initiating local command documented and certified the continued existence of a housing shortage. Effective June 12, 2024.

MAP 37-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 37-24(I), Clarify Eligibility Requirements for Non-Emergency Specialty Care
MAP 37-24(I).description: This item clarifies that a patient may be eligible for travel allowances if the qualifications as specified in health care programs established and administered by the Military Health System are met for travel reimbursement. Effective when published in the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR).

MAP 33-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 33-24(I), Update Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty OCONUS to Clarify Tour Length for Geilenkirchen, Germany
MAP 33-24(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside of the Continental United States (OCONUS). Personnel serving in a NATO position at Geilenkirchen NATO Air Base will continue to serve 36-month accompanied and 36-month unaccompanied tours. Personnel assigned to UICs FF6350 and FF4 I KO will serve a 36-month accompanied tour or a 24 month unaccompanied tour. Personnel (accompanied or unaccompanied) assigned to the NATO AWACS E-3A Component will serve

CAP 22-24.title: CTD for CAP 22-24(E), Shipment of Alternative Fueled POV or Circuitous Route to New PDS
CAP 22-24.description:
This item inserts clarifying language identified in General Service Administration’s
(GSA) Federal Travel Regulation (FTR) Case 2022-03, which issued a final ruling to allow
agencies greater flexibility for authorizing shipment of an alternative fueled privately-owned
vehicle (POV) or authorizing a circuitous route for these types of POVs to a new permanent duty
station (PDS). These changes will provide Services with additional factors to help determine
whether shipping a traveler’s alternative

MAP 25-24.title: UTD for MAP 25-24(R), Transport or Shipment of Human Breast Milk for PCS Travel
MAP 25-24.description: To provide a “special need” accommodation for a nursing Service member that allows for reimbursement of expenses associated with the transport or shipment of human breast milk while on travel for a permanent change of station (PCS) in accordance with Federal law. This authority would not extend to an accommodation for nursing Service members during an unaccompanied tour. Effective May 24, 2024.

CAP 18-24.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 18-24(E), Zero-Emission Vehicle Rental Vehicle Charging Related Expenses
CAP 18-24.description: This item provides a clarification to JTR Table 2-8 to include the following: (1) A provision under rule 4 that includes when the nearest compatible charging station has no charge points open and available as a reason the rental company’s refueling charges may reimbursed. (2) A rule to clarify a parking fee is reimbursable when the parking fee is a result of entering an airport parking lot or garage that offers free ZEV charging in order to charge the rental ZEV before return. Effective June 1,

MAP 96-23.title: UTD for MAP 96-23(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension – Bath, ME
MAP 96-23.description: This item reinstates the Bath, ME Military Housing Area (MHA) ME136 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in the Bath, ME MHA, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item reinstates Bath, ME as a location to receive up to 35 days TLE. Effective May 19, 2024.

CAP 27-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 27-24(I) Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 27-24(I).description: This item corrects miscellaneous minor grammatical errors in the Joint Travel Regulations. Effective date May 01, 2024.

CAP 21-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 21-24(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 21-24(I).description: This item corrects miscellaneous minor grammatical errors and updates the Table of Contents in the Joint Travel Regulations. Effective April 1, 2024.

MAP 26-24.title: UTD for MAP 26-24(R) Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension Recertification - Portsmouth, NH/Kittery, ME (NH194)
MAP 26-24.description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Portsmouth, NH/Kittery, ME (NH194) Military Housing Area. The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-dayperiod after the effective date.

MAP 24-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 24-24(I), Correction to Realign the JTR to Statutory Authority for the Career Intermission Program
MAP 24-24(I).description: This item realigns the JTR with the statutory authority contained in 10 USC §710 that entitles Service members participating in the program to "travel performed from the residence of the member, at the time of release from active service to participate in the program, to the location in the United States designated by the member as his residence during the period of participation in the program;...". The JTR originally stated "...HOS in the U.S..." but was inadvertently changed to “CONUS” in the

CAP 23-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 23-24(I), Partially Rescinding MAP-CAP 12-24 (I)
CAP 23-24(I).description: This item clarifies that due to review by the Office of General Counsel subsequent to the publication of MAP-CAP 12-24 (I) "Clarification of Rental Vehicle Expenses" it was identified that the changes made to Table 2-8 Rule 5 must be rescinded. Additional changes may be implemented once guidance is received regarding rental vehicle vendors charging the Government Administrative Rate Supplemental (GARS) after April 1, 2024 for reservations made prior to April 1, 2024 and how the U.S. Government w

MAP 101-23.title: UTD for MAP 101-23(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension – Vandenberg SFB, CA (CA026)
MAP 101-23.description: This item reinstates the Vandenberg SFB, CA Military Housing Area (MHA) CA026 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in the Vandenberg SFB, CAMHA, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item reinstates Vandenberg SFB, CA as a location to receive up to 60 days TLE.

CAP 14-24.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 14-24(E), Privately owned vehicle (POV) constructive cost analysis update
CAP 14-24.description: This item inserts clarifying language identified in GSA’s Federal register notice2023-00733, which issued a final ruling to clarify the calculation of “constructed cost” as it relates to temporary duty (TDY) travel. This item clarifies that what factors should be compared to privately owned vehicle costs to when preparing a constructive cost analysis. Prior Civilian Board of Contract Appeals (CBCA) and General Services Board of Contract Appeals(GSCBA)decisions also address the compiling of cost

MAP 19-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 19-24(I) Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification – Edwards AFB/Palmdale, CA (CA457)
MAP 19-24(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Edwards AFB/Palmdale CA (CA457) Military Housing Area (MHA). The initiating local command documented and certified the continued existence of a housing shortage.

MAP 15-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 15-24(I), Clarify Inactive Duty Training (IDT) Outside the Normal Commuting Area
MAP 15-24(I).description: This item clarifies that allowances paid to Reserve component (RC) members performing IDT outside the normal commuting distance are limited to expenses for travel to and from the IDT location (as identified in JTR, par. 032304-B).

CAP 12-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 12-24(I), Clarification of Rental Vehicle Expenses
CAP 12-24(I).description: This item clarifies rental vehicle expenses that are reimbursable based on the U.S. Government Rental Car Agreement #5 with an implementation date of April 1, 2024.

MAP 9-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 9-24(I) -- Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification – Atlantic City, NJ (NJ196)
MAP 9-24(I).description: This item extends the temporary lodging expense (TLE) extension recertification dates for the Atlantic City, NJ (NJ196) Military Housing Area (MHA). The initiating local command documented and certified the continued existence of a housing shortage on February 21, 2024.

CAP 13-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 13-24(I), Clarification of Electronic Travel System Authorization Authority
CAP 13-24(I).description: This item clarifies that travel authorizations produced in an electronic travel system are not required to use or reproduce a corresponding DD Form 1610 as long as the system contains all data elements required by FTR, Chapter 301, Appendix C. A printed copy of an electronic travel authorization is considered a valid order if it contains the data elements that would be included in a DD Form 1610.

CAP 8-24.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 8-24 (I) -- Add AFRICOM Funded Environmental and Morale Leave (FEML) Locations and Destinations
CAP 8-24.description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulation’s Authorized Funded Environmental and Morale Leave (FEML) Locations and Destinations supplement by designating five new AFRICOM locations.

CAP 05-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 05-24(I), Clarification of Limitation on Lodging Taxes and Fees Reimbursement
CAP 05-24(I).description: This item clarifies that if a traveler does not utilize lodging at or below the lodging portion of the applicable per diem rate, and is not authorized AEA, lodging taxes and percentage-based fees will be prorated to what the Government would have paid if the traveler did utilize lodging at the per diem rate. If the TDY location is an ILP location, the same applies to the published ILP rate rather than the per diem rate.

CAP 104-23(I).title: UTD-CTD for MAP-CAP 104-23(I), Sustainable Transportation for Official Temporary Duty (TDY) Travel
CAP 104-23(I).description: This is a statutory (S) item implementing the provisions of the Code of Federal Regulations Parts 301-10 and 301-70, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Memorandum M-24-05 “Catalyzing Sustainable Transportation Through Federal Travel,” and Executive Order 14057, “Catalyzing Clean Energy Industries and Jobs through Federal Sustainability.” FTR Bulletin 24-02, published in the Federal Register on December 18, 2023, states, “This bulletin clarifies existing regulations for determining the met

MAP 6-24(I).title: UTD for MAP 6-24(I) Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification – Moody AFB, GA (GA081)
MAP 6-24(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Moody AFB GA (GA081) Military Housing Area (MHA). The initiating local command documented and certified the continued existence of a housing shortage.

CAP 04-24(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 04-24(I), Remove Reference to Travel Modernization Pilot Program Exemption
CAP 04-24(I).description: This item removes the Travel Modernization Pilot Program exemption for using Defense Table of Distances.

MAP 14-23.title: UTD for MAP 14-23(R), Child Care Reimbursement Incident to a Permanent Change of Station (PCS)
MAP 14-23.description: This item implements a new pilot program pursuant by Section 627 of the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) for Fiscal Year (FY) 2023. In accordance with Section 627, the Secretary of Defense must carry out a pilot program to reimburse a member of the Armed Forces for travel and transportation expenses incurred by a child care provider during a permanent change of station (PCS) when child care is not available at a military child development center at the new permanent duty station (PDS) w

CAP 102-23(I).title: UTD-CTD for MAP-CAP 102-23(I), Add Language Clarifying Use of a Transportation Network Company (TNC)
CAP 102-23(I).description: This item clarifies and expands the use of a Transportation Network Company (TNC) in the Joint Travel Regulations.

MAP 82-23.title: UTD for MAP 82-23(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension –Portsmouth, NH/Kittery, ME Military Housing Area (MHA) NH194
MAP 82-23.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Navy requested a TLE extension for the Portsmouth, NH/Kittery, ME MHA (NH194) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing.

MAP 74-23.title: UTD for MAP 74-23(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension - Palmdale/Edwards AFB, CA (MHA CA457)
MAP 74-23.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, this item authorizes a TLE extension for the Palmdale/Edwards AFB, CA Military Housing Area (MHA) CA457 because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing.

CAP 110-23(I).title: UTD-CTD for MAP-CAP 110-23(I), Update DTMO Website Link and Evacuation Travel in Who May Use DTS
CAP 110-23(I).description: This item updates the current Defense Travel Management Office (DTMO) website link and the evacuation summary link with the frequently asked questions (FAQ) website link in Who May Use DTS.

CAP 109-23(I).title: UTD-CTD for MAP-CAP 109-23(I), Replace SF 1164 with OF 1164 and Update Table 2 in Defense Travel System (DTS) Regulations
CAP 109-23(I).description: This item replaces the Standard Form (SF) 1164 with the Optional Form (OF) 1164 and clarifies Defense Travel System (DTS) Regulations Table 2: Training.

CAP 107-23(I).title: MAP/CAP 107-23(I), CY2024 Government Meal Rates Frequently Asked Questions
CAP 107-23(I).description: This item revises the Government Meal Rates Frequently Asked Questions on the DTMO website.

CAP 106-23(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 106-23(I), CY2024 Privately Owned Vehicle (POV) Mileage Rates
CAP 106-23(I).description: This item announces the CY2024 POV Mileage Rates and updates the Frequently Asked Questions on the DTMO website for official temporary and relocation mileage rates. This change applies to travel and relocation performed on or after January 1, 2024.

MAP 108-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 108-23(I), CY2024 Dislocation Allowance (DLA) Rates
MAP 108-23(I).description: This item increases the DLA rates effective January 1, 2024 based on the basic rate of pay increase of 5.2% in the FY2024 President’s Budget. The FY2024 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) does not contain a provision to alter the increase rate.

CAP 88-23(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 88-23(I), Update Authorized R&R Locations and Destinations
CAP 88-23(I).description: This item adds eight additional countries designated as chargeable rest and recuperation (R&R) leave duty locations. The new locations designated as chargeable R&R leave duty locations are Algeria, Burundi, Cameroon (North and Far North), Libya, Mali, Niger, South Sudan, and Tunisia. These R&R locations are effective from November 30, 2023 through November 20, 2025.

MAP 86-23.title: UTD for MAP 86-23(R),Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension - AtlanticCity NJ (MHA NJ196)
MAP 86-23.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, this item authorizes a TLE extension for the Atlantic City, NJ Military Housing Area (MHA) NJ196 because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing. The authority for this action is 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in the Atlantic City, NJ military housing area, personnel require

MAP 81-23.title: UTD for MAP 81-23(R), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension – Moody AFB, GA (GA081)
MAP 81-23.description: This item reinstates the Moody AFB, GA (GA081) Military Housing Area (MHA)as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE). The authority for action is 37 U.S.C. § 452(b)(11). Due to a shortage of housing in the Moody AFB, GA MHA, personnel require additional time to locate and secure permanent housing. This item extends the end date through which Moody AFB, GA is a location where Service members may receive extended TLE. This item reinstates Moody AFB, GA as a location to re

MAP 99-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 99-23(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
MAP 99-23(I).description: This item makes administrative correction to the days identified in Table 5-15 from 10 to 14.

MAP 97-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 97-23(I) – Reduction of Extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Allowances When Receiving Basic Allowance for Housing Per Diem
MAP 97-23(I).description: This item clarifies that if the Service member is in receipt of per diem equal to the amount of the basic allowance for housing at the without-dependent rate, in accordance with par. 032201-B3 and Table 3-5, then the lodging portion of TLE is reduced to $0, from days 15 to 60, when the Service member is at a location approved for extended TLE.

MAP 98-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 98-23(I), Clarifying Active-Duty Service member as Attendant for Medical Specialty Care Travel of More Than 100 Miles
MAP 98-23(I).description: This item clarifies that to receive the standard travel and transportation allowances in Chapter 2, an eligible attendant must be an active-duty Service members and civilian employee.

MAP 95-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 95-23(I), Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Recertification – BATH, ME (ME136)
MAP 95-23(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Bath, ME (ME136) Military Housing Areas (MHA). The initiating local command documented and certified the continued existence of a housing shortage.

CAP 89-23(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 89-23(I), Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 89-23(I).description: This item removes Muster Duty information from JTR, par. 030302-C as this is governed by DoD FMR, Vol 7A, Chapter 58. This item also corrects miscellaneous minor errors throughout the JTR.

CAP 90-23(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 90-23(I), Additional Miscellaneous Corrections
CAP 90-23(I).description: This item corrects miscellaneous minor errors in MAP 85-23(S).

MAP 85-23.title: UTD for MAP 85-23(S), PCS Allowances when Attending Professional Education Training or Class
MAP 85-23.description: The FY23 National Defense Authorization Act, Section 625, directs the Department to authorize per diem in the amount of the basic allowance for housing without-dependent rate when the Service member is traveling to the professional military education or training location, on a PCS order, with orders to return to the duty station where the member maintains a primary residence and the dependent of the member resides.

MAP 91-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 91-23(I), Clarification of TLE Recertification and Extension Requirements
MAP 91-23(I).description: This item updates and clarifies documentation requirements for Local Authorization Process for TLE Extensions Due to a Housing Shortage.

CAP 87-23(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 87-23(I), Evacuation Authority derived from Department of State (DoS) cable
CAP 87-23(I).description: This item clarifies that a Department of State (DoS) cable authorizing or ordering departure of eligible family members under chief of mission from an embassy does authorize the Secretary of Defense to do the same without further consultations.

CAP 79-23.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 79-23(E), Clarification of Reimbursable Lodging Expenses Associated with Rental or Lease of Nonconventional Lodging such as a Single-Family Home or Apartment
CAP 79-23.description: This item clarifies JTR Paragraph 020303-G, indicating that rental or lease of a single-family home or apartment is considered long term lodging regardless of whether the lodging costs are accrued daily, weekly, or monthly. This item also clarifies Table 2-8,indicating that parking at a single-family home or apartment is considered part of the lodging cost and not separately reimbursable. This item also clarifies Table 2-16 fees, such as tourism, safe, service, or resort fees may only be reimbur

Impact_of_the_Potential_Government_Shutd.title: Impact of the Potential Government Shutdown on Official Travel
Impact_of_the_Potential_Government_Shutd.description: Travelers should take direction from their commands. In the event of a United States Government shutdown, the Defense Travel Management Office will be impacted.

MAP 72-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 72-23(I) TLE Extension Recertification Goldsboro/Seymour Johnson AFB, NC
MAP 72-23(I).description: Extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Seymour Johnson AFB NC (NC183) Military Housing Area (MHA). Effective August 23, 2023.

MAP 71-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 71-23(I) TLE Extension Recertification Holloman AFB NM
MAP 71-23(I).description: Extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Holloman AFB NM (NM205) MHA. Effective August 7, 2023.

MAP 21-23.title: UTD for MAP 21-23(R), Increase Recruiter Expenses
MAP 21-23.description: This item edits Joint Travel Regulations (JTR), pars. 020605 and 020606 to incorporate an increase to the reimbursement limits for recruiting expenses and monthly parking expenses.

MAP 69-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 69-23(I), Update Table 1-7 approval authority for U.S. Army
MAP 69-23(I).description: This item clarifies who may authorize and approve TDYs of 180 days or more for Army Service members in JTR, par. 010206, Table 1-7.

MAP 68-23.title: UTD for MAP 68-23(R) TLE Extension Recertification for Vandenberg SFB CA
MAP 68-23.description: Extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Vandenberg SFB CA (CA026) Military Housing Area (MHA). Effective July 20, 2023.

CAP 60-23.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 60-23(E), Travel In and Around the PDS
CAP 60-23.description: This item clarifies that for Service members and employees who are working remotely, whose official worksite is as defined in 5 CFR 531.602 and determined under the criteria in 5 CFR 531.605, whose remote official worksite is not within a defined local area, the local area will be within 50 miles of that remote official worksite. For remote Service members and employees within the local commuting area, transportation expenses between a Service member’s or an employee’s official worksite and the

MAP 67-23.title: UTD for MAP 67-23(R) TLE Extension Recertification - Moody AFB, GA
MAP 67-23.description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Moody AFB(GA081) Military Housing Area. The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date.

DoD_Approves_Pet_Expenses_Reimbursement_.title: DoD Approves Pet Expenses Reimbursement Due to PCS
DoD_Approves_Pet_Expenses_Reimbursement_.description: The Department of Defense has approved a new policy to cover pet travel expenses, like pet transportation or quarantine fees, incurred by Service members during a Permanent Change of Station (PCS).

MAP 02-23.title: UTD for MAP 02-23(R), Pet Expenses Due to a Permanent Change of Station
MAP 02-23.description: This item implements Sec 624 of FY 2023 NDAA which amends 37 U.S.C. 453 and authorizes reimbursement of costs related to the relocation of a pet that arise during a permanent change of station (PCS).  Under this new policy, a Uniformed Service member may be authorized reimbursement of up to $550 for a PCS within the continental United States and up to $2,000 for a PCS to or from a location outside the continental United States to cover expenses directly related to pet transportation, such as pet

CAP 53-23(I).title: CTD for CAP 53-23(I), Update Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty OCONUS to Remove Outdated Information
CAP 53-23(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, Tour Lengths and Tours of Duty Outside of the Continental United States (OCONUS) to remove outdated information regarding Air Force Management Interns. The Department of the Air Force no longer uses the Management Intern Program.

DoD_Ending_MyTravel_Program.title: DoD Ending MyTravel Program
DoD_Ending_MyTravel_Program.description: The Department will officially conclude MyTravel operations on September 13, 2023. All future DoD travel will be booked through the Defense Travel System (DTS).

CAP 50-23(I).title: CTD for CAP 50-23(I), Update Administrative Household Goods (HHG) Weight Allowance Locations Table for Poland and Romania
CAP 50-23(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, Administrative Household Goods (HHG) Weight Allowance Locations Table to rescind household goods weight allowance restrictions for Navy civilian employees assigned to Poland and Romania. Civilian employees assigned to Redzikowo, Poland and Deveselu, Romania are now authorized the full HHG weight allowance.

MAP 28-23.title: UTD for MAP 28-23(E) Add Pretoria, Johannesburg, and Cape Town, South Africa and Mbabane, Eswatini Authorized Consumable Goods Locations
MAP 28-23.description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, Authorized Consumable Goods Locations to authorize a Consumable Goods Allowance for Service members assigned to Pretoria, Johannesburg, and Cape Town, South Africa and Mbabane, Eswatini.

MAP 16-23.title: UTD for MAP 16-23(S), Transportation Reimbursement for Armed Forces Members Assigned to Alaska
MAP 16-23.description: This is a statutory (S) item implementing a mandatory provision of the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) for Fiscal Year (FY) 2023. Section 603 of the FY23 NDAA directs the Secretaries of the Armed Forces to reimburse eligible Armed Forces members the cost of airfare from the member’s permanent duty station (PDS) to their home of record when they are assigned to a PDS in Alaska and travel is authorized by an Officer above the grade of O-5. Effective between December 23, 2022 and December

UTD/CTD_for_MAP/CAP_\u2013_20-23(E),_Pre.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP – 20-23(E), Premium Class and Premium Economy Definition
UTD/CTD_for_MAP/CAP_\u2013_20-23(E),_Pre.description: This item edits JTR, pars. 020205, 031802, and Appendix A to incorporate the definition change of premium class accommodations, introduce premium economy airline accommodations as a class of service, and management controls related to the use premium class accommodations. Effective June 1, 2023.

MAP 46-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 46-23(I), TLE Extension Recertification - Edwards AFB/Palmdale, CA MHA (CA457)
MAP 46-23(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Edwards AFB/Palmdale, CA Military Housing Area (CA457). Effective 5/8/2023.

DoD_to_Change_the_Name_of_Nine_Army_Inst.title: DoD to Change the Name of Nine Army Installations by 2024
DoD_to_Change_the_Name_of_Nine_Army_Inst.description: The Department of Defense Naming Commission has renamed nine Army installations.

MAP 17-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 17-23(I), Update Administrative Household Goods (HHG) Weight Allowance Locations Table
MAP 17-23(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations Supplement, Administrative Household Goods (HHG) Weight Allowance Locations.

CAP 11-23(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 11-23(I) Add Language Clarifying Tip Reimbursement for Ground Transportation
CAP 11-23(I).description: This item clarifies tip reimbursement language in JTR, Table 2-13. If a traveler uses a Transportation Network Company, taxi, or limousine service, then the traveler may include a tip of up to 20% of the fare as part of the total fare amount claimed. The tip may not exceed 20% and it is not separately reimbursable. Fare means the price paid for transportation including all mandatory taxes and fees; it does not include ancillary fees for optional services. Effective March 1, 2023.

MAP 12-23(I).title: UTD for MAP 12-23(I) Clarify Re-delegation Authority
MAP 12-23(I).description: This item adds language to JTR, Table 3-2.1, clarifying the re-delegation authority for the Combatant Commands. The Combatant Commander may be re-delegate no lower than a two-star or civilian equivalent. Effective February 15, 2023.

MAP 04-23.title: UTD for MAP 04-23(R) Atlantic City, NJ TLE Extension
MAP 04-23.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the U.S. Coast Guard requested to reinstate a TLE extension for the Atlantic City, NJ military housing area (MHA) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing. Effective February 13, 2023.

MAP 07-23.title: UTD for MAP 07-23(R) Holloman AFB, NM TLE Extension
MAP 07-23.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Air Force requested a TLE extension for Holloman AFB, NM (NM205) military housing area (MHA) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing. Effective February 13, 2023.

MAP 94-22.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Air Force requested a TLE extension for Edwards AFB/Palmdale, CA (MHA CA457) because it has determined that the Military Housing Area is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing. MAP 93-22.title: UTD for MAP 93-22(R) Bath, ME (ME136) Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension
MAP 93-22.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Navy requested a TLE extension for Bath, ME (ME136) military housing area (MHA) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing.

MAP 97-22.title: UTD for MAP 97-22(R), Vandenberg SFB CA TLE Extension
MAP 97-22.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Air Force requested a TLE extension for Vandenberg AFB (CA026) military housing area (MHA) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing.

MAP 03-23.title: UTD for MAP 03-23(R) Moody AFB, GA (GA081) Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension
MAP 03-23.description: This item reinstates the Moody AFB Military Housing Area (MHA) GA081 as a location to receive an extended Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452. Due to the lingering effects of the pandemic, installations are experiencing a housing shortage, which is extending the time period for incoming personnel to find housing. This item reinstates the Moody AFB Military Housing Area (MHA) GA081 as a location to receive up to 60 days TLE.

CAP 01-23(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 01-23(I), Electronic Travel System Use of DD 1610
CAP 01-23(I).description: This item clarifies that travel authorizations or orders produced in an electronic travel system do not require the use of a DD Form 1610. A printout of the travel authorization is considered a valid order as long as it contains the required elements from the Federal Travel Regulation (FTR).

CAP 06-23(I).title: CTD for CAP 06-23(I), “Expiration of FTR Bulletin 22-07”
CAP 06-23(I).description: This item removes certain provisions from the JTR which implemented Federal Travel Regulations (FTR) Bulletin 22-07, “Relocation Allowances – Extended waiver of certain Federal Travel (FTR) provisions during the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic.” The bulletin temporarily waived certain provisions of the FTR governing official relocation travel and renewal agreement (RAT) travel during the COVID-19 pandemic. FTR Bulletin 22-07expired effective December 31, 2022.

2023_Mileage_Rates_+_FAQs.title: 2023 Mileage Rates + FAQs
2023_Mileage_Rates_+_FAQs.description: This item revises the Mileage Rates Frequently Asked Questions on the DTMO website to reflect revised calendar year (CY) 2023 standard mileage rates established by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS).

MAP 90-22.title: UTD for MAP 90-22(R) Portsmouth, NH/Kittery, ME (NH194) Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension
MAP 90-22.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Navy requested to reinstate a TLE extension for the Portsmouth, NH/Kittery, ME military housing area (MHA) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing.

MAP 101-22(I).title: UTD for MAP 101-22(I) 2023 Dislocation Allowance (DLA) Rates
MAP 101-22(I).description: This item increases the DLA rates effective January 1, 2023 based on the basic rate of pay increase of 4.6% in the FY2023 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA).

2023_Government_Meal_Rates_+_FAQs.title: 2023 Government Meal Rates + FAQs
2023_Government_Meal_Rates_+_FAQs.description: Government Meal Rates for 2023 calendar year, along with answers to frequently asked questions.

MAP 87-22.title: UTD for MAP 87-22(R) Shaw AFB/Sumter, SC (SD263) Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension
MAP 87-22.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Air Force requested a TLE extension for the Shaw AFB/Sumter, SC (SC263) military housing area (MHA) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing.

MAP 88-22.title: UTD for MAP 88-22(R), Seymour Johnson AFB/Goldsboro, NC Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension
MAP 88-22.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Air Force requested a TLE extension for the Seymour Johnson AFB/Goldsboro, NC (NC183) military housing area (MHA) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing.

MAP 84-22.title: UTD for MAP 84-22(R), Ellsworth AFB/Rapid City, SD Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) Extension
MAP 84-22.description: Under JTR, par. 050601-B3, the Air Force requested a TLE extension for the Ellsworth AFB/Rapid City, SD (SD264) military housing area (MHA) because it has determined that the MHA is experiencing a housing shortage that prevents Service members from obtaining adequate permanent Government-owned, Government-controlled, privatized or private-sector rental housing.

CAP 89-22(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 89-22(I) TMC Reimbursement for Rental Car and Lodging Reservations
CAP 89-22(I).description: This item adds rental car and lodging Travel Management Company fees to the Joint Travel Regulations as reimbursable expenses. New TMC contracts authorize TMC fees to be charged when booking lodging and rental car reservations.

2022_Per_Diem_Rate_Update_for_Alaska.title: 2022 Per Diem Rate Update for Alaska
2022_Per_Diem_Rate_Update_for_Alaska.description: PDTATAC staff members traveled to the state of Alaska and collected price data from restaurants most frequented by DoD travelers. Staff members then analyzed hotel and restaurant data and calculated updated rates. Effective November 1, 2022, per diem rates are updated for localities in the state of Alaska. A per diem bulletin was published in the Federal Register to notify agencies outside the DoD.

CAP 82-22(I).title: CTD for CAP 82-22(I) Waiver of Certain FTR Provisions in Connection with Official Relocation Travel
CAP 82-22(I).description: This item removes relocation allowance exceptions issued under GSA Bulletin FTR 22-02 and incorporates relocation allowance exceptions issued under GSA Bulletin FTR 23-03. Certain provisions of the Joint Travel Regulations (JTR) are temporarily waived for civilian employees relocating to locations in Florida, South Carolina, North Carolina, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico impacted by Hurricanes Ian and Fiona. The effective date for affected counties and tribal reservations in the state of Fl

CAP 81-22.title: CTD for CAP 81-22 Correction to Remove Lajes Field Portugal Civilian Accompanied Tour
CAP 81-22.description: In accordance with Acting Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness’s July 2, 2015 memorandum, the tour of duty for Department of Defense civilian employees assigned to Lajes Field, Portugal is revised to a 12-month unaccompanied tour. This change provides consistency and standardization of tours of duty for military and civilian personnel. This revision is effective on July 2, 2015.

MAP 11-22.title: UTD for MAP 11-22(R), Extended TLE Due to a Housing Shortage
MAP 11-22.description: In support of the Secretary of Defense’s initiative on “Taking Care of Our Service Members and Families,” this item introduces a new circumstance under which a temporary lodging expense (TLE) extension may be granted, outlines substantiating data framework, and modifies the eligibility process. Previously, under 37 U.S.C. § 474a, a Service member’s period of TLE at a location could be extended when that location is in an area covered by a Presidential declaration under the Stafford Act, or a mil

MAP 74-22.title: UTD for MAP 74-22(R), Temporary Lodging Expense Increase
MAP 74-22.description: As part of the Secretary of Defense’s initiative on “Taking Care of Our Service Members and Families,” the Secretary directed an increase in the standard time limitation for temporary lodging expense (TLE) from 10 days to 14 days during permanent change of station (PCS) moves in CONUS. Additionally, the 5-day time limitation during PCS moves from CONUS to OCONUS moves will be increased to 7 days.

MAP 75-22.title: UTD for MAP 75-22(R), Dislocation Allowance (DLA) 2022 Rate Increase
MAP 75-22.description: In support of the Secretary of Defense's initiative on "Taking Care of Our Service Members and Families," this item increases the Dislocation Allowance (DLA) rates for E-1 through E-4 by $500. Additionally, E-5 and E-6 rates will also be increased. These increases are effective 1 October, 2022. The current methodology of increasing all rates DLA rates based he annual basic pay increase will be effective January 1, 2023.

UTD_for_Multiple_Temporary_Lodging_Expen.title: UTD for Multiple Temporary Lodging Expense Extension Requests
UTD_for_Multiple_Temporary_Lodging_Expen.description: The following TLE extension requests have been approved: Bremerton, WA (MAP 65-22); Brunswick, ME (MAP 56-22); JB McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst, NJ (MAP 62-22); JB San Antonio, TX (MAP 67-22); Malmstrom AFB, Great Falls, MT (MAP 71-22); Moody AFB, GA (MAP 69-22); McConnell AFB/Wichita, KS (MAP 56-22); Cannon AFB/Clovis, NM (MAP 5622); Shaw AFB/Sumter SC (MAP 56-22); Ballston Spa/Albany, NY (MAP 56-22); Mountain Home AFB/Elmore County, ID (MAP 57-22); Luke AFB/Glendale, AZ (MAP 57-22); and Goodfellow AFB

CAP 61-22.title: UTD-CTD for MAP-CAP 61-22(E) Examples of When Travel Management Company Cannot be Reached
CAP 61-22.description: This item edits JTR, par. 020207 addressing the authority for an Authorizing Official to certify circumstances for reimbursement when a TMC cannot be reached.

MAP 66-22.title: UTD for MAP 66-22 (E) Adds Effective Date to Time Limitation for HHG in Connection with Retirement
MAP 66-22.description: This item adds an effective date to the time limitation for household goods (HHG) in connection with retirement. A Service member is eligible for the three-year time limitation if the active duty termination effective date is on or after June 24, 2022. MAP 75-21(R), “Change Time Limitation for Travel in Connection with Retirement” is effective on the date approved by the Chair and is not retroactive.

MAP 60-22.title: UTD for MAP 60-22(R) Dover, DE TLE Extension
MAP 60-22.description: This item establishes the Dover, DE Military Housing Area (MHA) DE054 as a location to receive an extension of Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) under the authority in 37 U.S.C. § 452. Due to the lingering effects of the pandemic, installations are experiencing a housing shortage, which is extending the time period for incoming personnel to find housing. Effective September 19, 2022.

UTD/CTD_MAP/CAP_\u2013_11-21(R).title: UTD/CTD MAP/CAP – 11-21(R)
UTD/CTD_MAP/CAP_\u2013_11-21(R).description: Purpose: This item creates a new miscellaneous reimbursable expense for Service members, civilian employees, and dependents who are required by a foreign host nation to quarantine within the PDS local area during a PCS. Effective: April 13, 2022

CAP 15-22.title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 15-22 (I) Update Funded Environmental and Morale Leave (FEML) Supplement
CAP 15-22.description: Purpose: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulation’s, Authorized Funded Environmental and Morale Leave (FEML) Locations and Destinations supplement by changing the table number location for eligibility requirements from Table 4-10 to Table 4-11. Effective: This revision is effective when published on the website at Authorized Funded Environmental and Morale Leave (FEML) Locations and Destinations.

MAP 02-22.title: UTD for MAP 02-22 (R) Houma, LA TLE Extension

MAP 32-26(I).title: UTD for MAP 32-26(I), TLE Recertification for Vandenberg SFB, CA (CA026)
MAP 32-26(I).description: This item extends the TLE extension recertification dates for the Vandenberg SFB, CA Military Housing Area (MHA CA026). The initiating local command must document and certify the continued existence of a housing shortage in the affected area within each 90-day period after the effective date. Failure to recertify the continued existence of a housing shortage will result in an early termination of the extension at the end of that 90-day period. Effective April 29, 2026.

CAP 27-26(I).title: UTD/CTD for MAP/CAP 27-26(I), Update DoD Text to DoW in Cover Letter, TOC, Ch 1, Ch 6, Appendix A
CAP 27-26(I).description: This item updates the Joint Travel Regulations from “Department of Defense,” “DoD,” “Secretary of Defense” to “Department of War,” “DoW,” “Secretary of War.” Effective May 1, 2026.
